commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer proof judgment or contributor-provided real behavior proof, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.; Cleared: The diff is limited to Control UI TypeScript projection/types and tests; it does not add dependencies, scripts, secrets handling, permissions, or supply-chain execution paths. (sha=8d7d85f15cba49e4c1f989be3bfbfb4f68ab57c5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads optional `toolCallId` through Control UI chat stream segment types, clamps matching tool-card sort timestamps behind their causal stream segment, and adds unit plus mocked-browser ordering coverage.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +123. Total +164 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main snapshots the assistant stream segment with local time and then uses a timestamp-only visible sort, so an older matching tool event can appear before the stream text that caused it. I did not run a live Gateway/WebChat reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer proof judgment or contributor-provided real behavior proof, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Live WebChat tool cards were reordered incorrectly when a tool event timestamp was older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call. The timestamp-only sorter could place tool cards *before* their causal assistant text.

## Why This Change Was Made
Thread optional `toolCallId` metadata through the chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep a matching tool card after its causal assistant text, while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering for everything else. `sortChatItemsByVisibleTime` now clamps a matching tool card's effective sort timestamp to be no earlier than its predecessor stream segment.

Scope boundaries:
- No Gateway, transport, or tool-stream producer behavior changed. The real WebChat path already writes `{ text, ts, toolCallId }` into `chatStreamSegments` in `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`; this PR only carries that existing field into the projection/sort layer.
- Ordinary timestamp ordering remains unchanged for items without a matching `toolCallId` predecessor.
- Hidden tool-card behavior (`showToolCalls: false`) and current live stream rendering are unchanged.

## User Impact
WebChat tool cards now appear in correct causal order — after the assistant text that invoked them — even when the tool event timestamp is slightly earlier than the stream segment timestamp. No behavior change for normal correctly ordered timestamps.

## Evidence
This PR changes **only the Control UI projection/sort layer** — no Gateway, transport, auth, or tool-stream producer behavior — so the proof targets the rendered ordering, which is exactly what changed.

Proofs 1–3 below were **re-run on PR head `8d7d85f15cba` (Node v22.22.0, Linux) for this update**. The browser e2e (proof 4) is described from its source and was **not re-run in this environment** (Chromium gate); it runs in CI.

### 1. Production projection output — real `buildChatItems` (re-verified)

Driving the real `buildChatItems` with the inversion scenario (tool ts `1000` older than the introducing stream segment ts `2000`):

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings <<'NODE'
import { buildChatItems } from './ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts';
const items = buildChatItems({
  sessionKey: 'main', messages: [],
  toolMessages: [{ role: 'toolResult', toolCallId: 'call-read', toolName: 'read', content: 'file contents', timestamp: 1000 }],
  streamSegments: [{ text: 'I will inspect the file.', ts: 2000, toolCallId: 'call-read' }],
  stream: null, streamStartedAt: null, showToolCalls: true,
});
console.log(items.map((i) => ('kind' in i && i.kind === 'stream') ? `stream:${i.text}` : `tool:${i.messages?.[0]?.message?.toolCallId}`));
NODE
```

Observed output — the assistant stream stays before its matching tool card despite the older tool timestamp:

```text
[ 'stream:I will inspect the file.', 'tool:call-read' ]
```

### 2. Regression with fix (re-verified)

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  45 passed (45)
```

### 3. Negative control (re-verified)

Reverting only `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts` to `origin/main` (new tests kept) fails exactly the two ordering tests, proving they guard the fix:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts
 × keeps a live tool card after the stream segment that introduced it
 × keeps same-millisecond stream segments interleaved with their matching tool cards
 Test Files  1 failed (1) / Tests  2 failed | 43 passed (45)
```

### 4. Real-browser e2e (in CI; not re-run in this environment)

`ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` → **"keeps live assistant stream text before the matching tool card"** drives the real `openclaw-app` Control UI in headless Chromium and reproduces the exact inversion bug:

- emits an assistant stream delta with a **current** timestamp (`"I will inspect the file."`);
- emits the matching `read` tool event with an **older** timestamp (`ts: Date.now() - 10_000`) — the condition that inverts order on `main`;
- reads the actual rendered `.chat-thread` DOM and asserts `expect(visibleOrder).toEqual(["assistant stream", "tool card"])`.

The Gateway in that test is a local mock that only *delivers* events; the event shapes (`assistant` delta + `agent`/`tool` start carrying `toolCallId`) match what the real producer emits (`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`), and the rendering/ordering under test is the real UI. This lane was not re-run locally here (Playwright Chromium gate); it executes in CI. A rendered screenshot from this e2e was attached earlier in the PR history.

### Why this is `proof: override`-eligible

This PR changes **only deterministic projection over `toolCallId` metadata the real path already emits** — no Gateway, transport, auth, or producer change (the producer writes `{ text, ts, toolCallId }` today; this PR carries that existing field into the sort layer). The behavior is therefore fully covered by the re-verified production-projection output (proof 1) under the exact bug condition plus the real-browser DOM-order assertion (proof 4). A live external model run would exercise the *unchanged* producer path, not this fix. Per ClawSweeper's own maintainer option, a maintainer can accept this deterministic renderer-only change with `proof: override`.

### Code quality
Production non-test delta is small and owner-scoped: `ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/app.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts` +46/-5. ClawSweeper rated patch quality `🦞 diamond lobster`.

### Not tested here
A live external Gateway with a real model/tool turn was not captured (no live model in this environment), and the Playwright e2e lane was not re-run locally; both exercise paths outside the projection change this PR makes.

Fixes #92122
